message:
fix(db): harden SQLite backup/recovery against symlink traversal

Replace is_file()/is_dir() calls with symlink_metadata-based
is_real_file()/is_real_directory() helpers throughout the SQLite
recovery pipeline to prevent symlink-based attacks:

- recover_sqlite_file: reject symlinked storage_root directories
- sqlite_backup_candidates: skip symlinked .bak files and symlinked
  entries in directory scans using entry.file_type() instead of
  path.is_file()
- restore_from_backup: refuse to restore from non-regular backup files
- ensure_sqlite_file_healthy_with_archive: validate both storage_root
  and its projects/ subdirectory are real directories, not symlinks
- Add quarantine_reconstructed_candidate() to properly quarantine
  empty or failed reconstruction attempts before returning errors,
  preventing stale empty databases from being left behind
- reconstruct_from_archive: early-return with warning when storage_root
  is missing or symlinked, avoiding database creation from untrusted
  directory trees

Adds tests for symlinked backup candidate filtering and symlinked
storage root rejection in archive recovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
